introduction.text: The UE shall have capability to follow the frame timing change of the reference cell in RRC_CONNECTED state or when transmitting PUSCH on CG resources for SDT in RRC_INACTIVE. The uplink frame transmission takes place $(N_{TA}+N_{TA offset})×T_{c}$ before the reception of the first detected path (in time) of the corresponding downlink frame from the reference cell.

clause_7_1_2.title: Requirements
clause_7_1_2.text: The UE initial transmission timing error shall be less than or equal to ±Te where the timing error limit value Te is specified in table 7.1.2-1.
conditions: when it is the first transmission in a DRX cycle for PUCCH, PUSCH and SRS, or it is the PRACH transmission, or it is the msgA transmission, or it is the first transmission sent on the PSCell for activating the deactivated SCG without RACH.
when it is the transmission for PUSCH on CG resources for SDT in RRC_INACTIVE.
when it is the first transmission on target cell after UE receives LTM cell switch command.

ssb_availability.UL SCS ≤ 120 kHz: at least one SSB is available at the UE during the last 160 ms
ssb_availability.UL SCS = 480 kHz: at least one SSB is available in the last 80 ms
ssb_availability.UL SCS = 960 kHz: at least one SSB is available in the last 40 ms

tables.title: Te Timing Error Limit
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

tables.title: The Value of $N_{TA offset}$
columns: Frequency range and band of cell used for uplink transmission
rows: FR1 FDD or TDD band with neither E-UTRA–NR nor NB-IoT–NR coexistence case
FR1 FDD band with E-UTRA–NR and/or NB-IoT–NR coexistence case
FR1 TDD band with E-UTRA–NR and/or NB-IoT–NR coexistence case
notes: Note 1: The UE identifies $N_{TA offset}$ based on the information n-TimingAdvanceOffset as specified in TS 38.331 [2]. If UE is not provided with the information n-TimingAdvanceOffset, the default value of $N_{TA offset}$ is set as 25600 for FR1 band. In case of multiple UL carriers in the same TAG, UE expects that the same value of n-TimingAdvanceOffset is provided for all the UL carriers according to clause 4.2 in TS 38.213 [3] and the value 39936 of $N_{TA offset}$ can also be provided for an FDD serving cell.
Note 2: Void

7.1.2.1.title: Gradual timing adjustment
rules: The maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change in one adjustment shall be Tq.
The minimum aggregate adjustment rate shall be Tp per second.
The maximum aggregate adjustment rate shall be Tq per 200 ms for SCS of UL signals smaller or equal to 120 kHz and 100 ms for SCS of uplink signals larger or equal to 480 kHz.

tables.title: Tq Maximum Autonomous Time Adjustment Step and Tp Minimum Aggregate Adjustment rate
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]
NOTE 2: When *highSpeedMeasFlagFR2-r17* is configured for UE supporting power class 6, K = 4.5; otherwise, K = 2.5.

7.1.2.3.title: One shot large UL timing adjustment for FR2 Power Class 6 UE

7.1.2.4.title: UE transmit timing for positioning measurements
7.1.2.4.text: If cell reselection occurs in RRC_INACTIVE within *srs-PosRRC-InactiveValidityArea area*, the UE shall have capability to follow the frame timing change of the reference cell when transmitting SRS for positioning.

tables.title: Te Timing Error Limit
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

rows: FR1 FDD or TDD band with neither E-UTRA–NR nor NB-IoT–NR coexistence case

FR1 TDD band with E-UTRA–NR and/or NB-IoT–NR coexistence case
notes: Note 1: The UE identifies $N_{TA offset}$ based on the information n-TimingAdvanceOffset as specified in TS 38.331 [2]. If UE is not provided with the information n-TimingAdvanceOffset, the default value of $N_{TA offset}$ is set as 25600 for FR1 band.
Note 2: Void

notes: NOTE: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

7.1A.2.2.title: UE transmit timing for positioning measurements
7.1A.2.2.reference: The requirements defined in clause 7.1.2.4 shall apply for this clause.

subclauses.title: UE transmit timing for Satellite Access

tables.title: Te_NTN Timing Error Limit
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

tables.title: Te_NTN Timing Error Limit for fixed VSAT is served by GSO and fixed VSAT is served by NGSO
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

tables.title: Te_NTN Timing Error Limit for mobile VSAT is served by GSO
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

NOTE 2: Te_NTN for 120 kHz UL SCS applies under all the following conditions: - LOS channel between UE and each GNSS satellite - The GNSS signal power level greater than or equal to the applicable value specified in Table 6.2-1 of TS 38.171 - The GNSS satellite allocation is no less than the applicable value specified in Table 6.2-2 of TS 38.171 - The velocity change within one second does not result in the position difference with respect to the projected position, based on the position and velocity obtained one second before, exceeding 10 meters.

tables.title: The Value of $N_{TA offset}$ for VSAT in FR2-NTN
columns: Frequency range and band of cell used for uplink transmission
notes: Note 1: The UE identifies $N_{TA offset}$ based on the information n-TimingAdvanceOffset as specified in TS 38.331 [2]. If UE is not provided with the information n-TimingAdvanceOffset, the default value of $N_{TA offset}$ is set as 0 for FR2-NTN band.

7.1C.2.1.title: Gradual timing adjustment
rules: The maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change, apart from a change of $N_{TA,adj}^{UE}$ due to satellite position update and $N_{TA,adj}^{common}$ between the previous transmission and the current transmission, in one adjustment shall be Tq_NTN.
The minimum aggregate adjustment rate, apart from a change of $N_{TA,adj}^{UE}$ due to satellite position update and $N_{TA,adj}^{common}$ during the last one second, shall be Tp_NTN per second.

For a FR1-NTN UE or a FR2-NTN fixed UE, the maximum aggregate adjustment rate, apart from a change of $N_{TA,adj}^{UE}$ due to satellite position update and $N_{TA,adj}^{common}$ during the last 200 ms, shall be Tq_NTN per 200 ms.

For a FR2-NTN mobile UE, the maximum aggregate adjustment rate, apart from a change of $N_{TA,adj}^{UE}$ due to satellite position update and $N_{TA,adj}^{common}$ during the last 200 ms, shall be 3 * Tq_NTN per 200 ms.

tables.title: Tq_NTN Maximum Autonomous Time Adjustment Step and Tp_NTN Minimum Aggregate Adjustment rate
notes: NOTE: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

tables.title: Te_ATG Timing Error Limit
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

7.1D.2.1.title: Gradual timing adjustment
rules: The maximum amount of the magnitude of the timing change, apart from a change of $N_{TA,adj}^{UE}$ due to ATG UE position update, in one adjustment shall be Tq_ATG.
The minimum aggregate adjustment rate, apart from a change of $N_{TA,adj}^{UE}$ due to ATG UE position update, shall be Tp_ATG per second.
The maximum aggregate adjustment rate, apart from a change of $N_{TA,adj}^{UE}$ due to ATG UE position update, shall be Tq_ATG per 200 ms.

tables.title: Tq_ATG Maximum Autonomous Time Adjustment Step and Tp_ATG Minimum Aggregate Adjustment rate
notes: NOTE 1: Tc is the basic timing unit defined in TS 38.211 [6]

tables.title: UE Timer Accuracy
notes: The requirements are only related to the actual timing measurements internally in the UE.
They do not include: Inaccuracy in the start and stop conditions of a timer, or Inaccuracies due to restrictions in observability of start and stop conditions of a UE timer.

tables.title: UE Timer Accuracy for RedCap
notes: The requirements are only related to the actual timing measurements internally in the UE.
They do not include: Inaccuracy in the start and stop conditions of a timer, or Inaccuracies due to restrictions in observability of start and stop conditions of a UE timer.

subclauses.title: UE timer accuracy for satellite access

clause_7_2C_2.title: Requirements
clause_7_2C_2.applicability: The requirements in this clause are applicable for both UE served by SAN in FR1 and VSAT UE served by SAN in FR2-NTN.

tables.title: UE Timer Accuracy for Satellite Access
notes: The requirements are only related to the actual timing measurements internally in the UE.
They do not include: Inaccuracy in the start and stop conditions of a timer, or Inaccuracies due to restrictions in observability of start and stop conditions of a UE timer.

tables.title: ATG UE Timer Accuracy
notes: The requirements are only related to the actual timing measurements internally in the ATG UE.
They do not include: Inaccuracy in the start and stop conditions of a timer, or Inaccuracies due to restrictions in observability of start and stop conditions of a ATG UE timer.

7.3.2.1.title: Timing Advance adjustment delay
7.3.2.1.text: UE shall adjust the timing of its uplink transmission timing at time slot n+ k+1 for a timing advance command received in time slot n, and the value of k is defined in clause 4.2 in TS 38.213 [3].

tables.title: UE Timing Advance adjustment accuracy

7.3A.2.1.title: Timing Advance adjustment delay
7.3A.2.1.text: UE shall adjust the timing of its uplink transmission at time slot n+ k+1 for a timing advance command received in time slot n, and the value of k is defined in clause 4.2 in TS 38.213 [3].

tables.title: UE Timing Advance adjustment accuracy

7.3C.2.1.title: Timing Advance adjustment delay
7.3C.2.1.text: UE shall adjust the timing of its uplink transmission timing from the beginning of uplink time slot n+ k+1+2µ ∙ K_{offset} for a timing advance command received in time slot n, and the value of k, µ and K_{offset} are defined in clause 4.2 in TS 38.213 [3].

tables.title: UE Timing Advance adjustment accuracy
UE Timing Advance adjustment accuracy for VSAT UE in FR2-NTN
notes: NOTE 1: VSAT UE are defined in TS 38.101-5 [43]

7.3D.2.1.title: Timing Advance adjustment delay
7.3D.2.1.text: ATG UE shall adjust the timing of its uplink transmission timing from the beginning of uplink at time slot n+ k+1+2µ ∙ K_{offset} for a timing advance command received in time slot n, and the value of k, µ and K_{offset} are defined in clause 4.2 in TS 38.213 [3].

tables.title: ATG UE Timing Advance adjustment accuracy
columns: ATG UE Timing Advance adjustment accuracy

definition.text: Cell phase synchronization accuracy for TDD is defined as the maximum absolute deviation in frame start timing between any pair of cells on the same frequency that have overlapping coverage areas.

minimum_requirements.text: The cell phase synchronization accuracy measured at BS antenna connectors or radiated interface boundaries shall be better than 3 µs.

introduction.text: A UE shall be capable of handling a relative transmission timing difference between subframe timing boundary of E-UTRA PCell and the closest slot timing boundary of PSCell to be aggregated for EN-DC operation.

tables.title: Maximum uplink transmission timing difference requirement for asynchronous EN-DC
columns: UL Sub-carrier spacing for data in PSCell (kHz)
notes: NOTE 1: For E-UTRA FDD-NR FDD intra-band EN-DC, for which the requirement is defined in clause 7.5.3 and this Table 7.5.2-1 is also applicable, the scenario with 120 kHz PSCell does not exist.

notes: NOTE 1: This is not applicable for a UE which indicates the capability of only supporting single UL timing (*ul-TimingAlignmentEUTRA-NR* is signalled). Single UL timing for E-UTRA and NR cell is assumed for this UE.

NOTE 2: If the transmission timing difference exceeds the cyclic prefix length of the UL Sub-carrier spacing for data in PSCell, NR UE Tx EVM degradation is expected for the symbol that is overlapping the LTE subframe boundary

enabled.text: When *deriveSSB-IndexFromCell* is enabled, the UE assumes frame boundary alignment (including half frame, subframe and slot boundary alignment) across cells on the same frequency carrier is within a tolerance not worse than:
conditions: min (2 SSB symbols, 1 PDSCH symbol) for sub-carrier spacings of SSB and PDSCH up to 240 kHz
min (3 SSB symbols, NPDSCH PDSCH symbols) for sub-carrier spacing of 480 kHz and 960 kHz of either SSB or PDSCH where NPDSCH is defined in table 7.7.1-1

tables.title: NPDSCH when deriveSSB-IndexFromCell is enabled

disabled.text: When *deriveSSB-IndexFromCell* is not enabled, the UE assumes frame boundary alignment (including half frame, subframe and slot boundary alignment) across cells on the same frequency carrier is within a tolerance not worse than 6 SSB symbols for sub-carrier spacing of 960 kHz and the SFNs of all cells on the same frequency carrier are the same.

enabled.text: When *deriveSSB-IndexFromCell* is enabled, the RedCap UE assumes frame boundary alignment (including half frame, subframe and slot boundary alignment) across cells on the same frequency carrier is within a tolerance not worse than min(2 SSB symbols, 1 PDSCH symbol) and the SFNs of all cells on the same frequency carrier are the same.

When *deriveSSB-IndexFromCell* is enabled, the ATG UE assumes frame boundary alignment (including half frame, subframe and slot boundary alignment) across cells on the same frequency carrier is within a tolerance not worse than min(2 SSB symbols, 1 PDSCH symbol) and the SFNs of all cells on the same frequency carrier are the same.

minimum_requirements.note: UE does not expect the *deriveSSB-IndexFromCell* to be set as true when frame boundary across detectable cells on the same frequency carrier is larger than the minimum tolerance requirement.

enabled.text: When *deriveSSB-IndexFromCellInter-r17* is enabled, the UE assumes frame boundary alignment (including half frame, subframe and slot boundary alignment) across cells on the target carrier and reference cell is within a tolerance not worse than min(2 SSB symbols of target carrier, 1 PDSCH symbol of the reference cell) and the SFNs of all cells on the target carrier and reference cell are the same.
enabled.reference_cell: The reference cell is the serving cell which is used for SSB indexes derivation as indicated via *deriveSSB-IndexFromCellInter-r17*.

enabled.text: When *deriveSSB-IndexFromCellInter-r17* is enabled, the ATG UE assumes frame boundary alignment (including half frame, subframe and slot boundary alignment) across cells on the target carrier and reference cell is within a tolerance not worse than min (2 SSB symbols of target carrier, 1 PDSCH symbol of the reference cell) and the SFNs of all cells on the target carrier and reference cell are the same.
enabled.reference_cell: The reference cell is the serving cell which is used for SSB indexes derivation as indicated via *deriveSSB-IndexFromCellInter-r17*.

minimum_requirements.note: UE does not expect the *deriveSSB-IndexFromCellInter-r17* to be set as true when frame boundary across detectable cells on the target carrier and reference cell is larger than the minimum tolerance requirement.
